projectDescription: A multi-source AI research agent that orchestrates parallel live searches across Google, Bing, and Reddit via Bright Data, then synthesizes results using GPT-4 into cited, structured answers.
keyFeatures: Parallel multi-source search across Google, Bing, and Reddit simultaneously
Asynchronous snapshot handling with polling and exponential backoff via Bright Data
Layered LLM analysis: per-source summaries followed by cross-source synthesis
LangGraph orchestration with typed state, deterministic node wiring, and retries
Structured Pydantic outputs with citations and provenance for every claim
Modular, production-grade design extensible to additional data sources
publicApiSurface: run_chatbot() — interactive CLI entry point for submitting research queries
graph.invoke(state) — programmatic graph invocation with typed State dict
serp_search(query, engine) — Bright Data SERP scraping for Google and Bing
reddit_search_api(keyword, date, sort_by, num_of_posts) — Reddit keyword search via Bright Data snapshots
reddit_post_retrieval(urls, days_back, load_all_replies, comment_limit) — fetch Reddit post comments
poll_snapshot_status(snapshot_id, max_attempts, delay) — async snapshot readiness polling
download_snapshot(snapshot_id, format) — download completed Bright Data snapshot
get_google_analysis_messages(user_question, google_results) — prompt builder for Google analysis
get_bing_analysis_messages(user_question, bing_results) — prompt builder for Bing analysis
get_reddit_analysis_messages(user_question, reddit_results, reddit_post_data) — prompt builder for Reddit analysis
get_synthesis_messages(user_question, google_analysis, bing_analysis, reddit_analysis) — prompt builder for final synthesis
State TypedDict — typed shared state schema for the LangGraph workflow
RedditURLAnalysis Pydantic model — structured output schema for URL selection
